Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Relief From Violation Of Temporary Restraining Order Or, In The Alternative, December 7 And December 14 Minute Orders Re Real Property Temporary Restraining Order; Memorandum In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Seth Cox, 310.640.3070 of Wedgewood, Inc for Defendant Redwood Holdings. (Court call)
Pamela Jackson, 707.446.2333 of Pamela Jackson Inc. for Redwood Holdings. (Zoom)
Matthew Mellen, 510.263.8441 of the Mellen Law firm for Nancy McGinnis. (Zoom)
Elizabeth Cermak, 510.263.8441 of the Mellen Law firm for Nancy McGinnis. (Zoom)
Nicholas Karkazis, 916.447.0700 of Stole Rives, LLP for Defendant Select Profolio Servicing Inc. (Zoom)

Argued. Mr. Mellen argues that since there was a continuance than the TRO should have not expired and should have remained in effect.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF NANCY MGGINNIS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Relief From Violation Of Temporary Restraining Order Or, In The Alternative, December 7 And December 14 Minute Orders Re Real Property Temporary Restraining Order is DENIED. TRO expired by its own terms before December 31, 2020. Unlike in Wutchumna Water Co. v. Superior Court in and for Tulare County (1932) 215 Cal. 734, 736, Plaintiff fails to present any record or other evidence that the Court announced its intention to grant an extension of the TRO/injunction, i.e. "at the conclusion of the hearing the judge merely said: 'the injunction will issue. Bond in the sum of $75,000. Usual conditions of course." The Court also notes that Plaintiff has not requested the return of the bond ordered in conjunction with the TRO, which was requested after years of non-payment on the loan due to the beneficiary's alleged failure to send periodic statements, which payments Plaintiff was ready, willing and able to make. (Second Amended Complaint, paragraphs 11, 12, 30 and 33). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
